nativeRange.habitat: Reported by hobby vendors to originate from the Belem region of northern Brazil; precise wild habitat is undocumented in scientific literature. The genus Limnophila is otherwise predominantly Old World (tropical Asia, Africa, Australia), so the South American origin is contested in the trade.

decorPreferences: moderate to high lighting for compact form and coloration

summary: A rare, fine-leaved Brazilian stem plant in the same genus as Rice Paddy Herb (Limnophila aromatica). Forms densely branched, bushy stems with narrow serrated leaves that flush bright green to purple-pink at the tips under high light, CO2, and iron-rich fertilization.
careNotes: Limnophila sp. 'Belem' is a relatively rare aquarium stem plant in the family Plantaginaceae, named after the Belem region of northern Brazil where hobby vendors report it was originally collected. The South American origin is contested — the genus Limnophila is predominantly Old World (tropical Asia, Africa, Australia), and several major retailers (Buce Plant, Pet Zone SD) list it as native to Asia while others (Aquascape Supply, Glass Aqua) list it as Brazilian; no formal scientific description has been published, hence the cultivar designation 'Belem'. It is closely related to Limnophila aromatica (Rice Paddy Herb, fw-plant-007) but has noticeably finer, narrower, serrated leaves and a more compact, densely branching habit. Sources broadly agree on a temperature range of about 22-28 °C (72-84 °F) and pH 6.0-7.5; hardness is generally tolerated across soft to moderately hard water but is not specifically reported. Adult height is reported inconsistently across sources — Buce Plant and Pet Zone SD cite 4-8 in (~10-20 cm), Aquascape Supply cites 2-4 in (~5-10 cm) as a foreground/carpet plant, and search snippets describe stems reaching 30 cm under high light with CO2 — averaged here to 10-30 cm to span the realistic submersed range, recognising that growth stays compact under lower light. For best appearance — fine bright green to purple-pink foliage with vivid growing tips — high light (~30-50 PAR), pressurized CO2 (~25-30 mg/L), nutrient-rich aquasoil, and a balanced micro/macro fertilization regime (including iron) are recommended. The plant survives without CO2 but stays plainer green and grows more slowly. Like other Limnophila, it is sensitive to sudden parameter swings and tissue-culture-to-submersed transitions, and can melt if shocked; introduce gradually and trim regularly to encourage bushy lateral branching.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: trim anywhere along an established stem (typically 5-10 cm of newest growth with several leaves) and replant directly into the substrate. The plant branches readily, so cutting the top of a mature stem encourages multiple lateral side shoots from the mother plant, producing a denser bush over time.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used for family (Plantaginaceae), care level (moderate), light (high), CO2 (required for optimal growth/coloration), growth rate (medium), placement (midground/background), pH (6.0-7.5), temperature (72-84 °F / 22-29 °C), adult size (4-8 in / 10-20 cm), propagation (herbaceous stem cuttings), and coloration notes (purples/pinks at growing tips under high light + CO2). Buce Plant lists native range as Asia; this conflicts with other vendors and the manifest's Brazilian-Belem origin and is flagged in careNotes.

additional.name: Aquascape Supply
additional.notes: Cross-checked Brazilian origin ('originates from Belem, Brazil, where it was first discovered in the wild'), high light, CO2 beneficial, nutrient-rich substrate, midground/foreground placement, and coloration (vibrant green with reddish hue under high light). Reports a smaller adult size of 2-4 in (~5-10 cm) and describes a dense carpet-like effect in groups — used to anchor the lower bound of the adult size range.

additional.name: Pet Zone SD
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Plantaginaceae), care level (moderate), high light, CO2 required, medium growth rate, background/midground placement, pH 6.0-7.5, temperature 72-84 °F, adult size 4-8 in, herbaceous stem-cutting propagation, and purple/pink coloration at growing tips. Also notes Asia as origin (conflicting with Brazilian source) — flagged in careNotes.

additional.name: Glass Aqua
additional.notes: Cross-checked care level (moderate to advanced), medium-to-high light, CO2 recommended (survives without but better with), moderate-to-fast growth rate, midground/background placement, nutrient-dense aquasoil substrate, branches readily and needs regular pruning, propagation by trimming and replanting stems. Recommended for medium-to-large planted tanks.
